steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Locate point L on the circle
steps.content:
$L = (-3, y)$ lies on the circle $x^2 + y^2 = 9$:
$$(-3)^2 + y^2 = 9 \Rightarrow y^2 = 0 \Rightarrow y = 0$$
So $L = (-3, 0)$, directly opposite $J = (3, 0)$ across the center $K = (0, 0)$.

steps.title: Find all possible positive measures of angle JKL
steps.content:
$J$ and $L$ are diametrically opposite, so the central angle between them is $\pi$ radians. Adding any whole number of full rotations gives the general form:
$$\angle JKL = \pi + 2\pi k = (2k+1)\pi \text{ for integer } k \ge 0$$
Valid measures are odd integer multiples of $\pi$: $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, \dots, 43\pi, \dots$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘$\frac{45\pi}{2}$’: $\frac{45\pi}{2} = 22.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$. Eliminate.
  • Choice B — ‘$\frac{47\pi}{2}$’: $\frac{47\pi}{2} = 23.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — ‘$42\pi$’: $42\pi$ — an even multiple of $\pi$, which corresponds to landing back at $J$, not at $L$. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — ‘$43\pi$’: $43\pi = (2 \cdot 21 + 1)\pi$ — an odd multiple of $\pi$, valid.
That confirms D is correct.
